questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Select the correct article: 'The supervisor is ____ honest engineer who never compromises on safety.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The choice between 'a' and 'an' depends on the sound, not the spelling, of the following word.

**Working.** 'Honest' begins with a silent 'h', so it starts with a vowel sound, requiring 'an'.

**Answer.** an.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The area of a rectangle of length $12$ and width $5$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Area of rectangle $=\text{length}\times\text{width}$.

**Working.** $12\times 5 = 60$.

**Answer.** $60$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Compute the perimeter of the rectangle shown with length $8$ and width $3$.

figure.caption: Rectangle
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='40' y='40' width='120' height='50' fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'/><text x='95' y='105' font-size='8'>l=8</text><text x='12' y='68' font-size='8'>w=3</text></svg>

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the option that correctly completes the sentence: 'By the time the rescue team arrived, the trapped miners ____ for nearly ten hours.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** An action continuing up to a point in the past uses the past perfect continuous tense.

**Working.** The reference point ('the team arrived') is in the past, and the waiting was ongoing before it. This requires 'had been + verb-ing'.

**Answer.** had been waiting.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The average of $10, 20, 30, 40$ and $50$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Average = sum divided by count.

**Working.**
$$\frac{10+20+30+40+50}{5}=\frac{150}{5}=30$$

**Answer.** $30$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: From $4$ engineers and $3$ geologists, a committee of $3$ is to be formed with exactly $2$ engineers. The number of ways is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Multiply independent choices.

**Working.** $\binom{4}{2}\times\binom{3}{1}=6\times3=18$.

**Answer.** $18$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A mine increased its ventilation rate, and the measured methane concentration dropped. The most reasonable inference is that
options: better ventilation reduced the gas concentration
ventilation has no effect on gas
the gas caused the ventilation to increase
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Choose the inference best supported by a plausible mechanism.

**Working.** Increased airflow dilutes and removes methane, lowering its concentration.

**Answer.** better ventilation reduced the gas concentration.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A invests $5000$ and B invests $7000$ in a business for the same period. A profit of $6000$ is shared in the ratio of their investments. B's share is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Profit splits in the ratio of capital (equal time).

**Working.** Ratio $=5:7$; B's share $=6000\times\dfrac{7}{12}=3500$.

**Answer.** $3500$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Which arrangement best represents the relationship among Dog, Cat and Animal?
options: Two separate circles (Dog and Cat) lying inside one larger circle (Animal)
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dogs and cats are disjoint subclasses of animals.

**Working.** Dog and Cat are non-overlapping sets, both contained within Animal.

**Answer.** Two separate circles inside a larger Animal circle.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Identify the part that contains an error: 'One of the pumps (A) / are not working (B) / properly in (C) / the lower level. (D)'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The phrase 'one of the + plural noun' takes a singular verb because the true subject is 'one'.

**Working.** The subject is 'one', so the verb should be 'is not working', not 'are not working'. The error lies in part B.

**Answer.** B.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A pillar has a strength of 40 MPa and carries an average stress of 16 MPa (as shown). Compute its factor of safety.

figure.caption: Pillar loading
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='50' y='50' width='60' height='90' fill='#94a3b8' stroke='#475569'/><g stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2'><line x1='60' y1='30' x2='60' y2='48' marker-end='url(#pl)'/><line x1='80' y1='30' x2='80' y2='48' marker-end='url(#pl)'/><line x1='100' y1='30' x2='100' y2='48' marker-end='url(#pl)'/></g><defs><marker id='pl' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='4' refY='6' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L4 6 L8 0 Z' fill='#dc2626'/></marker></defs><text x='30' y='160' font-size='8'>strength 40, stress 16 MPa</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $FoS = \dfrac{\text{strength}}{\text{stress}}$.

**Working.**
$$FoS = \frac{40}{16} = 2.5$$

**Answer.** $\mathbf{2.5}$.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: Using the unit values shown (coal selling price ₹50/t, production cost ₹20/t, stripping cost ₹6 per unit), compute the break-even stripping ratio (BESR).

figure.caption: Unit economics
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='130' x2='200' y2='130' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='50' y='40' width='30' height='90' fill='#16a34a'/><text x='42' y='32' font-size='7'>price 50</text><rect x='100' y='80' width='30' height='50' fill='#f59e0b'/><text x='96' y='72' font-size='7'>cost 20</text><rect x='150' y='110' width='30' height='20' fill='#dc2626'/><text x='144' y='102' font-size='7'>strip 6</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** The break-even stripping ratio is the overburden that the net coal value can just pay to remove.

**Working.**
$$BESR = \frac{\text{price} - \text{cost}}{\text{stripping cost}} = \frac{50 - 20}{6} = 5$$

**Answer.** $\mathbf{5}$ (mining is economic while actual SR < 5).

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.topic: Time Value of Money — Sinking Fund
questions.stem: How much must be deposited at the end of each year for $5$ years at $10\%$ to accumulate $\textsf{Rs}\,1{,}00{,}000$? $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ .

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: Overcoring with a strain cell is a technique used to measure
options: the in-situ stress state in rock
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Stress relief on overcoring lets a recovered core expand.

**Working.** Measured relief strains are converted to the in-situ stress tensor.

**Answer.** the in-situ stress state in rock.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: An airway of cross-section $12\,\text{m}^2$ carries air at $2.5\,\text{m/s}$. The air quantity is _____ m$^3$/s.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $Q=A\,v$.

**Working.**
$$Q=12\times2.5=30.$$

**Answer.** $30\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: With $\lambda=6$/hr and $\mu=10$/hr, the average waiting time in the queue $W_q$ is _____ hours. Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $W_q=\dfrac{\lambda}{\mu(\mu-\lambda)}$.

**Working.**
$$W_q=\frac{6}{10(10-6)}=\frac{6}{40}=0.15.$$

**Answer.** $0.15$ hour ($9$ min).

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: Replacement theory is used to determine
options: the optimum time to replace equipment that deteriorates with age
the cut-off grade of an ore body
the critical path of a project
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Replacement balances rising maintenance cost against capital recovery.

**Working.** The optimum age minimises average annual cost.

**Answer.** the optimum time to replace deteriorating equipment.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan has a static pressure of $480\,\text{Pa}$, passes $40\,\text{m}^3$/s and draws $24\,\text{kW}$ at the shaft. Its static efficiency is _____ percent.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\eta_s=\dfrac{P_s Q}{\text{shaft power}}$.

**Working.**
$$\eta_s=\frac{480\times40}{24000}=\frac{19200}{24000}=0.80.$$

**Answer.** $80\%$.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: The figure shows a row of holes fired with increasing inter-hole delay along the row. The main purpose of using delays between holes is to:
options: provide free faces for later holes and reduce ground vibration
increase the total charge detonating per instant
eliminate the burden ahead of each hole

figure.caption: Delayed initiation sequence
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 90' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='#dc2626'><circle cx='30' cy='50' r='6'/><circle cx='80' cy='50' r='6'/><circle cx='130' cy='50' r='6'/><circle cx='180' cy='50' r='6'/></g><text x='20' y='30' font-size='8'>0 ms</text><text x='68' y='30' font-size='8'>25 ms</text><text x='118' y='30' font-size='8'>50 ms</text><text x='168' y='30' font-size='8'>75 ms</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Delay blasting.

**Reasoning.** Firing holes in sequence lets earlier holes create a new free face for later holes, improving fragmentation, and it limits the maximum charge per delay, which reduces peak ground vibration.

**Answer.** Provide free faces for later holes and reduce ground vibration.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: In rotary drilling, rock is broken mainly by
options: crushing and shearing under high axial thrust combined with rotation of the bit
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Rotary drilling uses thrust and torque.

**Reasoning.** High pulldown (thrust) presses the tricone or drag bit into the rock while rotation makes the cutters crush/shear it; cuttings are removed by flushing. It is the dominant method for large-diameter opencast blastholes.

**Answer.** Crushing/shearing under thrust and rotation.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Driving an additional airway in parallel with an existing district
options: removes the need for a fan
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Parallel paths offer more area for flow.

**Working.** Equivalent resistance falls.

**Answer.** reduces the overall resistance.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For $f(x,y)=x^{3}y^{2}$, the mixed partial derivative $\dfrac{\partial^{2}f}{\partial x\,\partial y}$ at $(1,1)$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Differentiate successively; order does not matter for this smooth function.

**Working.**
$$\frac{\partial f}{\partial y}=2x^{3}y,\qquad \frac{\partial}{\partial x}\left(2x^{3}y\right)=6x^{2}y$$
At $(1,1)$: $6(1)(1)=6$.

**Answer.** $6$.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: The stope shown is mined in horizontal slices, each mined-out slice being backfilled before the next is taken. This method is:

figure.caption: Backfilled stope
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='40' y='20' width='100' height='160' fill='#fde9c8' stroke='#92400e'/><rect x='40' y='120' width='100' height='60' fill='#cbd5e1'/><text x='90' y='155' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>backfill</text><text x='90' y='90' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>ore</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Cut-and-fill replaces each mined slice with fill before the next lift.

**Reasoning.** Mining in successive horizontal lifts with backfill providing the working floor and wall support is cut-and-fill stoping, suited to weak walls and high-value ore.

**Answer.** Cut-and-fill stoping.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: Opportunity cost is
options: the value of the best alternative forgone
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Choosing one option means giving up the next-best.

**Working.** Its forgone value is the opportunity cost.

**Answer.** the value of the best alternative forgone.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: At 240 m depth with an overburden unit weight of 25 kN/m³, the vertical stress, in MPa, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\sigma_v=\gamma H$.

**Working.**
$$\sigma_v=25\times240=6000\ \text{kPa}=6\ \text{MPa}$$

**Answer.** 6 MPa.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Three airways of resistance $1$, $2$ and $3\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$ are in series. The equivalent resistance is _____ Ns$^2$/m$^8$.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $R_{eq}=\sum R_i$.

**Working.**
$$1+2+3=6.$$

**Answer.** $6\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: With a 12-min cycle, the number of trips a dumper makes per hour is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Trips/h $=\dfrac{60}{\text{cycle time (min)}}$.

**Working.**
$$n=\frac{60}{12}=5$$

**Answer.** 5 trips/h.

questions.subject: mine-surveying
questions.stem: Two stations $100\ \text{m}$ apart differ in level by $5\ \text{m}$ (figure). Compute the gradient (%).

figure.caption: Gradient between stations
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='100' x2='200' y2='60' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='30' y1='100' x2='200' y2='100' stroke='#475569'/><text x='42' y='118' font-size='8'>rise 5 m over 100 m</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Gradient $=\dfrac{\text{rise}}{\text{distance}}\times100$.

**Working.**
$$g=\frac{5}{100}\times100=5\%$$

**Answer.** $5\%$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A body starts from rest with uniform acceleration $2\,\text{m/s}^2$. The distance travelled in $5\,\text{s}$ is _____ m.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $s=ut+\tfrac12 at^2$ with $u=0$.

**Working.**
$$s=\tfrac12(2)(5)^2=25.$$

**Answer.** $25\,\text{m}$.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: A project NPV of $\textsf{Rs}\,13{,}724$ at $10\%$ has, after $5$ years, an equivalent future worth of $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ . Round to the nearest rupee.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: Which of the following are continuous (rather than cyclic) excavating machines?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Continuous machines dig without a discrete load-swing-dump cycle.

**Reasoning.** The bucket wheel excavator, surface miner and bucket chain excavator all excavate in a continuous stream. A rope shovel is the classic cyclic (discontinuous) digger.

**Answer.** BWE, surface miner and bucket chain excavator.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Three airways of resistance $0.2$, $0.3$ and $0.5\ \mathrm{Ns^2/m^8}$ are joined in series. Compute the equivalent resistance ($\mathrm{Ns^2/m^8}$).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** In series, resistances add: $R_{eq}=R_1+R_2+R_3$.

**Working.**
$$R_{eq}=0.2+0.3+0.5=1.0$$

**Answer.** $1.0\ \mathrm{Ns^2/m^8}$.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: A bed of true thickness $30\ \text{m}$ dips at $30^\circ$ and outcrops on flat ground (figure). Compute the outcrop width measured perpendicular to strike (m).

figure.caption: Outcrop of a dipping bed
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='40,120 160,120 120,40' fill='#a16207' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='40' y1='130' x2='160' y2='130' stroke='#2563eb'/><text x='58' y='145' font-size='8'>outcrop width?</text><text x='92' y='92' font-size='8'>t=30, dip 30 deg</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** On flat ground $w=\dfrac{t}{\sin\delta}$.

**Working.**
$$w=\frac{30}{\sin30^\circ}=\frac{30}{0.5}=60\ \text{m}$$

**Answer.** $60$ m.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: A dewatering pump must overcome a static lift of $80\ \text{m}$ plus friction losses equivalent to $20\ \text{m}$ of head. Compute the total pumping head (m).

figure.caption: Static lift plus friction head
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='40' y1='140' x2='40' y2='30' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='34' y1='80' x2='46' y2='80' stroke='#94a3b8'/><text x='50' y='115' font-size='8'>static=80 m</text><text x='50' y='58' font-size='8'>friction=20 m</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Total head $=$ static head $+$ friction head.

**Working.**
$$H=80+20=100\ \text{m}$$

**Answer.** $100$ m.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A block of mass $10\ \mathrm{kg}$ rests on a horizontal surface with coefficient of friction $\mu=0.3$ (see figure). The maximum friction force that can be developed, in N, is _____ (two decimals; take $g=9.81\ \mathrm{m/s^2}$).

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='20' y1='100' x2='200' y2='100' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='80' y='70' width='50' height='30' fill='#b45309'/><line x1='130' y1='85' x2='185' y2='85' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#fr)'/><defs><marker id='fr' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#2563eb'/></marker></defs><text x='150' y='78' font-size='9' fill='#2563eb'>pull</text><text x='66' y='120' font-size='9'>m=10 kg, mu=0.3</text></svg>
figure.caption: Block on a horizontal surface

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $F=\mu N=\mu m g$.

**Working.**
$$F=0.3\times10\times9.81=29.43\ \text{N}$$

**Answer.** $29.43$ N.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: To keep the scaled distance at least 20 m/kg$^{0.5}$ at a structure 100 m away, the maximum charge per delay, in kg, must not exceed ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** From $SD=D/\sqrt{W}$, $W=(D/SD)^2$.

**Working.**
$$W_{max}=\left(\frac{100}{20}\right)^2=5^2=25\ \text{kg}$$

**Answer.** 25 kg.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For $f(x)=\sin x$, the value of the fifth derivative $f^{(5)}(0)$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Maclaurin series $\sin x=x-\dfrac{x^{3}}{3!}+\dfrac{x^{5}}{5!}-\cdots$; coefficient of $x^{5}$ is $\dfrac{f^{(5)}(0)}{5!}$.

**Working.** The coefficient of $x^{5}$ is $\dfrac{1}{5!}$, so $f^{(5)}(0)=5!\cdot\dfrac{1}{5!}=1$.

**Answer.** $1$.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: A coal-dust explosion in a mine is typically
options: initiated by a firedamp ignition or blast that raises and ignites deposited coal dust, then propagates along the airways
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Coal dust explosions usually follow a primary ignition.

**Reasoning.** A firedamp flame or blown-out shot raises settled coal dust into a cloud and ignites it; the explosion then feeds on further dust along the roadways, becoming far-reaching and devastating.

**Answer.** Firedamp/blast raises and ignites deposited dust.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For $y'=y$ with $y(0)=1$ and step $h=0.1$, TWO Euler steps give $y(0.2)\approx$ _____ (round to 2 decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Apply forward Euler twice.

**Working.**
$$y_1=1+0.1(1)=1.1,\qquad y_2=1.1+0.1(1.1)=1.21$$

**Answer.** $1.21$.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: Removing $50{,}00{,}000$ tonnes of waste at $\textsf{Rs}\,50$/t costs $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Cost $=$ tonnage $\times$ unit cost.

**Working.**
$$5000000\times50=250000000.$$

**Answer.** $\textsf{Rs}\,25$ crore.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A uniaxial test gives an axial stress of $50\ \text{MPa}$ at an axial strain of $0.002$ in the elastic range (figure). Compute Young's modulus (GPa).

figure.caption: Stress-strain line
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='30' x2='30' y2='110' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='30' y1='110' x2='200' y2='110' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='30' y1='110' x2='160' y2='40' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2'/><text x='35' y='28' font-size='8'>stress 50 MPa</text><text x='72' y='125' font-size='8'>strain 0.002</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $E=\dfrac{\sigma}{\varepsilon}$.

**Working.**
$$E=\frac{50}{0.002}=25000\ \text{MPa}=25\ \text{GPa}$$

**Answer.** $25$ GPa.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: An airway of resistance $0.2\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$ has a pressure drop of $80\,\text{Pa}$. The quantity flowing is _____ m$^3$/s.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $Q=\sqrt{P/R}$.

**Working.**
$$Q=\sqrt{\frac{80}{0.2}}=\sqrt{400}=20.$$

**Answer.** $20\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Which of the following is ALWAYS shared by a matrix $A$ and its transpose $A^{T}$?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\det(A^{T}-\lambda I)=\det\big((A-\lambda I)^{T}\big)=\det(A-\lambda I)$, so they share the characteristic polynomial.

**Working.** Equal characteristic polynomials $\Rightarrow$ identical eigenvalues, but the eigenvectors generally differ.

**Answer.** Eigenvalues.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: For ventilation airways connected in series, the equivalent resistance is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Series airways carry the same quantity; their pressure drops add.

**Working.** $R_{eq}=\sum R_i$.

**Answer.** $R_{eq}=R_1+R_2+\dots$.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: A common treatment for acid mine drainage is
options: neutralisation with lime/limestone to raise pH and precipitate the dissolved metals (often with settling/wetlands)
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Raise pH to drop out metals.

**Reasoning.** Alkaline reagents (lime, limestone) neutralise the acidity; the rise in pH precipitates iron and other metals as hydroxides, which settle out in ponds or constructed wetlands.

**Answer.** Lime/limestone neutralisation and metal precipitation.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: In a forcing auxiliary ventilation system, fresh air is delivered to the face
options: through a duct with the fan at the fresh-air (intake) end
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A forcing system blows air to the face.

**Working.** The fan draws intake air and forces it down the duct.

**Answer.** through a duct with the fan at the fresh-air end.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: With 34 m³ loose per cycle and 60 cycles per hour, the dragline's loose output, in m³/h, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Loose hourly output $=$ load per cycle $\times$ cycles/h.

**Working.**
$$Q_L=34\times60=2040\ \text{m}^3/\text{h}$$

**Answer.** 2040 m³/h (loose).

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: The tail (balance) rope hung beneath the conveyances of a friction winder
options: balances the changing weight of head rope as the conveyances move, keeping winder torque nearly constant
questions.solution:
**Concept.** As one conveyance rises, the head rope on its side shortens while the other lengthens, creating an unbalanced rope weight.

**Reasoning.** A tail rope of equal mass hung in a loop beneath the conveyances compensates this changing head-rope weight, so the out-of-balance torque (and motor load) stays nearly constant through the wind.

**Answer.** Balances head-rope weight for constant torque.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: At a depletion rate of $\textsf{Rs}\,20$/tonne, the depletion charge for a year mining $50{,}000$ tonnes is $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Charge $=$ rate $\times$ output.

**Working.**
$$20\times50000=1000000.$$

**Answer.** $\textsf{Rs}\,10{,}00{,}000$.

questions.subject: Mine Economics & Planning
questions.stem: Annual demand is $D=10{,}000$ units, ordering cost is $\textsf{Rs}\,200$/order and holding cost is $\textsf{Rs}\,4$/unit/yr. From the inventory saw-tooth model, compute the economic order quantity (units).

figure.caption: Inventory saw-tooth (EOQ)
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='110' x2='220' y2='110' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='30' y1='110' x2='30' y2='20' stroke='#475569'/><polyline points='30,30 80,110 80,30 130,110 130,30 180,110' fill='none' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2'/><text x='95' y='130' font-size='8'>order quantity Q</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\text{EOQ}=\sqrt{\dfrac{2DS}{H}}$.

**Working.**
$$\text{EOQ}=\sqrt{\frac{2\times10000\times200}{4}}=\sqrt{1{,}000{,}000}=1000$$

**Answer.** $1000$ units.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: Which condition MOST promotes spontaneous heating of coal?
options: A high-rank anthracite seam in a dry, well-swept airway
Crushed, low-rank, high-pyrite coal left in the goaf with just enough air leakage to supply oxygen but not remove heat
Strong through-ventilation that removes all heat
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Heating needs reactive coal, oxygen supply and poor heat removal.

**Reasoning.** Friable low-rank coal with pyrite has a large reactive surface; loose coal in the goaf with slow air leakage gets oxygen for oxidation but the trickle of air cannot carry away the heat, so it accumulates.

**Answer.** Crushed low-rank/pyritic coal in a leaky goaf.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: Backfill placed in a cut-and-fill stope primarily
options: supports the walls, provides a working platform and improves regional ground stability
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Fill is a structural element of the method.

**Reasoning.** It confines the wall rock (raising its strength), gives a stable floor for the next lift, and limits convergence/subsidence across the mine. Cemented fill can even stand as a free wall for adjacent extraction.

**Answer.** Wall support, platform and regional stability.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Using the trapezoidal rule with $2$ sub-intervals ($h=1$), the estimate of $\displaystyle\int_{0}^{2}x^{2}\,dx$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $T=\dfrac{h}{2}\big[f_0+2f_1+f_2\big]$.

**Working.** $f(0)=0,\,f(1)=1,\,f(2)=4$.
$$T=\frac{1}{2}\big[0+2(1)+4\big]=3$$

**Answer.** $3$ (the exact value is $\tfrac{8}{3}$; the trapezoidal rule over-estimates here).

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A block (see figure) is on the point of sliding down a rough plane inclined at $30^\circ$. The coefficient of static friction is _____ (two decimals).

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><path d='M20 140 L200 140 L200 60 Z' fill='#e5e7eb' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='120' y='90' width='28' height='20' fill='#b45309' transform='rotate(-24 134 100)'/><path d='M170 140 A40 40 0 0 0 158 113' fill='none' stroke='#dc2626'/><text x='150' y='134' font-size='10' fill='#dc2626'>30°</text><text x='52' y='128' font-size='9'>block on verge of sliding</text></svg>
figure.caption: Block on a rough incline

questions.solution:
**Concept.** At the verge of sliding, $\mu=\tan\theta$ (angle of repose).

**Working.**
$$\mu=\tan 30^\circ=0.577\approx0.58$$

**Answer.** $0.58$.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: A negative net present value indicates that the project
options: earns less than the required rate of return and should be rejected
questions.solution:
**Concept.** NPV $<0$ means discounted benefits fall short of cost.

**Working.** The project fails the MARR test.

**Answer.** earns less than the required rate of return and should be rejected.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: A 'toe' in bench blasting refers to
options: unbroken rock left standing at the bench floor when sub-drilling or burden is inadequate
the stemming column at the collar
the free face of the bench
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Toe is bottom rock that fails to break.

**Reasoning.** Insufficient bottom charge (too little sub-drill or too large a burden) leaves a ridge of hard rock at floor level — the toe — which obstructs loading and raises the floor.

**Answer.** Unbroken rock at the bench floor.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan (see figure) delivers $Q=40\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$ at a pressure of $800\ \mathrm{Pa}$. The air power, in kW, is _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='70' cy='70' r='40' fill='#e0f2fe' stroke='#475569'/><g stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='3'><line x1='70' y1='70' x2='70' y2='38'/><line x1='70' y1='70' x2='98' y2='86'/><line x1='70' y1='70' x2='42' y2='86'/></g><line x1='112' y1='70' x2='200' y2='70' stroke='#16a34a' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#ff)'/><defs><marker id='ff' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#16a34a'/></marker></defs><text x='120' y='60' font-size='9'>Q = 40 m^3/s</text><text x='120' y='100' font-size='9'>p = 800 Pa</text></svg>
figure.caption: Main fan duty

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Air power $=p\,Q$.

**Working.**
$$AP=800\times40=32000\ \text{W}=32\ \text{kW}$$

**Answer.** $32$ kW.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Carbon monoxide is especially dangerous because it
options: combines with haemoglobin and prevents oxygen transport in blood
is heavier than air and settles
questions.solution:
**Concept.** CO has a high affinity for haemoglobin.

**Working.** It forms carboxyhaemoglobin, starving tissues of oxygen.

**Answer.** it combines with haemoglobin, preventing oxygen transport.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: Vertical crater retreat (VCR) mining breaks ore by
options: spherical (crater) charges placed in large-diameter downholes, blasting horizontal slices that retreat upward, ore drawn from below
questions.solution:
**Concept.** VCR applies crater-blasting theory to large downholes.

**Reasoning.** Concentrated spherical charges at optimum depth crater the ore into horizontal slabs; the stope retreats upward, broken ore being drawn from drawpoints below — no personnel enter the stope.

**Answer.** Spherical charges in downholes blasting upward-retreating slices.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: A $\textsf{Rs}\,1{,}00{,}000$ investment yields a constant annual profit of $\textsf{Rs}\,25{,}000$. The simple rate of return is _____ percent.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Simple ROR $=\dfrac{\text{annual profit}}{\text{investment}}$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{25000}{100000}=0.25=25\%.$$

**Answer.** $25\%$.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: The threshold limit value (TLV-TWA) of a contaminant is
options: the concentration that is instantly fatal
the time-weighted average concentration to which a worker may be exposed over a normal 8-hour day/40-hour week without adverse effect
the explosive limit of the gas
the concentration that extinguishes a flame
questions.solution:
**Concept.** TLV-TWA is an occupational exposure benchmark.

**Reasoning.** It represents the average airborne concentration over a working shift believed safe for nearly all workers on repeated exposure; short-term and ceiling limits supplement it.

**Answer.** Safe 8-hour time-weighted average concentration.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: In shrinkage stoping,
options: the stope is filled with imported backfill after each slice
part of the broken ore is left in the stope as a working platform and temporary wall support, only fully drawn at the end
the ore is drawn completely after each blast
no ore is broken until the stope is complete
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The broken muck pile itself is the support and floor.

**Reasoning.** Miners work upward standing on the broken ore; because broken ore swells, a portion (~one-third) is drawn after each blast to keep working height, and the remaining ~two-thirds is drawn only after the stope is fully mined.

**Answer.** Broken ore retained as platform/support, drawn at the end.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: For the line in the previous problem (departure +50.0 m), the easting of the next station, in m, is ____ (one decimal).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** New easting $=$ old easting $+$ departure.

**Working.**
$$1000.0+50.0=1050.0\ \text{m}$$

**Answer.** 1050.0 m.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: Sulphur dioxide (SO₂) in mine air is typically associated with
options: the combustion or blasting of sulphide-bearing ore/coal, giving a pungent irritant gas
questions.solution:
**Concept.** SO₂ comes from sulphur oxidation.

**Reasoning.** Burning or blasting sulphide ores/high-sulphur coal releases SO₂, a pungent, acidic, irritant gas that attacks the eyes and respiratory tract.

**Answer.** Combustion/blasting of sulphide ore.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The largest eigenvalue of $A=\begin{bmatrix}2 & 1\\ 1 & 2\end{bmatrix}$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Solve the characteristic equation $\det(A-\lambda I)=0$.

**Working.**
$$(2-\lambda)^2-1=0\;\Rightarrow\;2-\lambda=\pm 1\;\Rightarrow\;\lambda=1,\,3$$

**Answer.** Largest eigenvalue $=3$.
